contract.name: Seal and Flatten
contract.description: Place the current piece to complete one or more full rows, clearing them and leaving the surface flatter. Use it whenever you can remove lines without creating overhangs or new holes, especially early

contract.name: Tuck L Left
contract.description: Rotate and slide the L piece left to tuck it under an overhang, plugging a left-side cavity without raising the stack. Use it in midgame defense when the board has left-facing gaps and you need to red

contract.name: Fill S Gap
contract.description: Fill S Gap means guiding an S piece into a stepped notch—two adjacent cells offset by one row—to seal a small zigzag cavity. Use it when your stack leaves that exact S-shaped opening, especially in mi

contract.name: Smooth the Surface
contract.description: Flatten uneven columns by placing pieces to fill dips and reduce jagged height differences, even if it doesn’t clear lines immediately. Use it in midgame when the stack is moderate and holes exist, so

contract.name: Place for flatness
contract.description: Place pieces to keep the surface as flat and even as possible, avoiding tall spikes or deep wells. Use this on routine placements in Tetris, especially early or when no line clear/setup is available,

contract.name: Keep Stack Low
contract.description: In Tetris, keep the stack flat and low by placing early pieces to fill shallow gaps instead of building towers. Use this in the opening and whenever the board is clean to preserve space and flexibilit

contract.name: Stabilize the Stack
contract.description: Use this when the stack is getting messy or hole-filled: place pieces to flatten peaks, cap exposed cavities, and preserve a clean vertical well for future I-pieces. Prioritize safe, low-risk placemen
